seoTitle: Oxley vs Oxley Island — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Oxley and Oxley Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Oxley vs Oxley Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Oxley and Oxley Island.
heading: Oxley vs Oxley Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Oxley, New South Wales and Oxley Island, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Oxley has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Oxley Island (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Oxley or Oxley Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Oxley has a flood risk score of 22/100, while Oxley Island scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Oxley scores 22/100 for bushfire risk. Oxley Island scores 25/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Oxley or Oxley Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Oxley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.65% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
